_source: Largest Christian denominations and Muslim groups in Nigeria by membership/prominence — compiled from Pew Research, denominational records and public reporting. National reference; local presence varies by city.

topChristianDenominations.name: Roman Catholic Church
topChristianDenominations.note: Nigeria's single largest Christian body, strong in the South-East and Middle Belt.

topChristianDenominations.name: Anglican Communion (Church of Nigeria)
topChristianDenominations.note: Widespread, historically strong in the South and Middle Belt.

topChristianDenominations.name: Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG)
topChristianDenominations.note: The largest Pentecostal church, with parishes in almost every neighbourhood.

topChristianDenominations.name: Living Faith Church (Winners' Chapel)
topChristianDenominations.note: Major Pentecostal church known for large auditoriums.

topChristianDenominations.name: Mountain of Fire and Miracles (MFM)
topChristianDenominations.note: Prominent Pentecostal ministry, strong in the South-West.

topChristianDenominations.name: Christ Embassy (LoveWorld)
topChristianDenominations.note: Fast-growing Pentecostal church with a big media presence.

topChristianDenominations.name: Assemblies of God
topChristianDenominations.note: Established Pentecostal denomination, strong in the East.

topMuslimGroups.name: Sunni (mainstream)
topMuslimGroups.note: The large majority of Nigerian Muslims, especially across the North.

topMuslimGroups.name: Tijaniyya (Sufi order)
topMuslimGroups.note: Widespread Sufi brotherhood, strong in the North-West.

topMuslimGroups.name: Qadiriyya (Sufi order)
topMuslimGroups.note: Historic Sufi brotherhood present across the North.

topMuslimGroups.name: Izala (JIBWIS)
topMuslimGroups.note: Prominent reformist movement, influential in the North.

topMuslimGroups.name: NASFAT
topMuslimGroups.note: Large Yoruba Muslim organisation, prominent in the South-West.
